name: RAG-AI-Scientist-OGTS-Benchmark
description: Execution-grounded evaluation suite for retrieval-augmented scientific code assistants. Contains two complementary benchmarks: (1) OGTS (Oracle-Guided Tree Search) — 50 deterministic micro-tasks across four families of domain-specific scientific computing problems (AMS closed-form, weighted log-loss, nDCG@k, threshold scan for AMS), each with 6 pre-computed oracle test cases and numeric tolerances; and (2) RAG Gold Queries — 500 retrieval evaluation queries with path-pattern relevance labels, difficulty tags (easy/medium/hard), and optional required_terms for lexical grounding, designed to evaluate retrieval quality over an indexed physics document corpus.
citeAs: Anonymous. RAG AI Scientist: Retrieval Grounding for Scientific Code Assistants, with an ATLAS Higgs-Challenge Replication Testbed. NeurIPS 2026 Evaluations & Datasets Track (submitted).
rai:dataLimitations: The OGTS task suite covers only four families of scientific computing micro-tasks (AMS, log-loss, nDCG, threshold scan); broader coverage of tensor manipulation, data wrangling, and visualization tasks would strengthen generalizability claims. The RAG gold queries are specific to the ATLAS Higgs ML Challenge corpus and may not transfer to other scientific domains without re-labeling. Oracle tolerances are set to 1e-9 (numeric_close), which is strict and may penalize numerically valid but differently-rounded implementations. Not recommended for evaluating multi-file code generation, interactive debugging, or tasks requiring external libraries.
rai:dataBiases: Task distribution is skewed toward physics-domain metrics (AMS, threshold scan) with 26/50 OGTS tasks in this family. The RAG query set was synthetically generated from a seed set of 10 hand-authored queries via paraphrase expansion, so query diversity is bounded by the seed topics. All tasks use English prompts only. The oracle test cases use uniformly sampled random inputs, which may not reflect the distribution of real-world inputs in production pipelines.
rai:personalSensitiveInformation: This dataset contains no personal or sensitive information. All data is synthetic (OGTS tasks with random numeric inputs) or derived from publicly available physics challenge documentation (RAG queries referencing the ATLAS Higgs ML Challenge 2014, CERN Open Data Portal).
rai:dataUseCases: Primary intended use: benchmarking LLM code-generation accuracy on domain-specific scientific computing tasks, comparing search strategies (linear retry vs. tree search) under oracle verification. Secondary: evaluating retrieval quality of RAG systems over indexed scientific corpora. Validity has been established for pass@k evaluation of Python code generation models and MRR/nDCG/Recall@k evaluation of vector-store retrieval. Not validated for: general-purpose code generation, natural language understanding, or tasks outside the physics/ML metrics domain.
rai:dataSocialImpact: Positive: promotes transparent, reproducible evaluation of AI coding assistants in scientific domains, helping researchers trust and verify AI-generated code. Encourages oracle-based verification over self-reported accuracy. Negative risks are minimal given the synthetic nature of the data; however, high pass rates on this benchmark should not be interpreted as evidence of general scientific coding competence.
rai:dataCollection: OGTS micro-tasks were procedurally generated (Python, fixed random seed) with numeric oracles derived from reference implementations. RAG gold queries were seeded from 10 expert-authored English prompts and deterministically paraphrased to 500 variants; path relevance labels are pattern-based heuristics aligned to the indexed ATLAS Higgs challenge documentation corpus.

prov:wasGeneratedBy.description: Data collection: deterministic code generation (seed=1337) for OGTS tasks; template-based paraphrase expansion for RAG queries from 10 seeds. Preprocessing: none on released JSONL artifacts beyond generation-time validation. Annotation: RAG relevance is defined by substring path patterns and optional lexical required_terms (no crowd labeling; expanded paraphrases were not human-reviewed). Tools: Python 3.11, NumPy, scikit-learn (optional ATLAS pipeline replication).

distribution.name: ogts_50_tasks.jsonl
distribution.description: 50 OGTS execution-grounded tasks with oracle test cases

distribution.name: rag_queries_500_neurips_mirror.jsonl
distribution.description: 500 retrieval evaluation queries with relevance labels and difficulty tags

recordSet.name: ogts_tasks
recordSet.description: Each record is one execution-grounded micro-task with a prompt, entrypoint function name, and oracle_kind. Full oracle payloads (cases, tolerances, and expected outputs) are JSON objects stored in the `oracle_payload` key of each line in ogts_50_tasks.jsonl; load the JSONL directly for oracle-grounded evaluation.

field.name: title
field.description: Task family name (e.g., AMS closed-form, Weighted log-loss)

field.name: prompt
field.description: Natural-language prompt instructing the model to write a Python module

field.name: entrypoint
field.description: Name of the Python function the generated module must define

field.name: oracle_kind
field.description: Type of oracle verification: numeric_close, numeric_equal, or json_equal

recordSet.name: rag_queries
recordSet.description: Each record is one retrieval evaluation query with relevance labels for measuring RAG system quality.

field.name: difficulty
field.description: Difficulty label: easy, medium, or hard

field.name: relevant_path_patterns
field.description: Substring patterns that must appear in retrieved chunk metadata for the chunk to be counted as relevant

field.name: required_terms
field.description: Optional lexical terms that should appear in concatenated top-k chunk text for groundedness checks

field.name: benchmark_segment
field.description: Stratification tag for query subsets (e.g., positive control segment)

field.name: seed_id
field.description: Identifier of the hand-authored seed query this row was expanded from

field.name: seed_run
field.description: Optional pointer to the provenance run artifact used when generating this mirror file
